symptoms: Fever, chills, body aches, fatigue, cough, sore throat, runny nose
precautions: Rest, hydration, avoid contact with others; annual flu vaccine recommended

symptoms: Fever, dry cough, fatigue, loss of taste/smell, breathlessness
precautions: Isolation, mask use, hydration, monitor O2 saturation; vaccination strongly advised

symptoms: Persistent cough >3 weeks, hemoptysis, night sweats, weight loss, fever
precautions: Complete full course; DOT (Directly Observed Therapy) recommended; avoid alcohol

symptoms: High fever, productive cough, chest pain, dyspnea, fatigue
precautions: Rest, hydration, follow-up chest X-ray; hospitalize if SpO2 <94%

symptoms: Cyclical fever, chills, sweating, headache, vomiting, anemia
precautions: Avoid mosquito exposure, use nets and repellents; test before treatment

symptoms: High fever, severe headache, retro-orbital pain, rash, myalgia, thrombocytopenia
precautions: AVOID Aspirin/NSAIDs (bleeding risk); monitor platelets; IV fluids if needed

symptoms: Sustained fever, abdominal pain, rose spots, constipation/diarrhea, headache
precautions: Boil drinking water, hand hygiene; typhoid vaccine available

symptoms: Profuse watery diarrhea ('rice water'), vomiting, rapid dehydration
precautions: Strict water/food hygiene; IV fluids for severe cases; cholera vaccine

symptoms: Jaundice, fatigue, abdominal pain, dark urine, nausea
precautions: Avoid alcohol; hepatitis B vaccine for contacts; monitor liver function

symptoms: Fatigue, jaundice, right upper quadrant pain, dark urine
precautions: Avoid alcohol; no vaccine available; screen close contacts

symptoms: Acute: flu-like illness; Chronic: weight loss, recurrent infections, night sweats
precautions: Condom use, no needle sharing, PrEP for prevention; regular CD4/viral load monitoring

symptoms: Primary: painless chancre; Secondary: rash, flu-like; Tertiary: organ damage
medicine: Benzathine Penicillin G 2.4 million units IM
precautions: Partner notification and treatment; avoid sexual contact until healed

symptoms: Urethral/vaginal discharge, dysuria, pelvic pain; often asymptomatic in women
precautions: Treat partner simultaneously; screen for co-infections; avoid unprotected sex

symptoms: Often asymptomatic; dysuria, discharge, pelvic inflammatory disease
precautions: Treat partner; abstain from sex for 7 days post-treatment

symptoms: Hypopigmented skin patches, loss of sensation, peripheral nerve thickening
precautions: Complete full MDT course; prevent disability; educate family; notify contacts

symptoms: Sudden severe headache, stiff neck, photophobia, fever, petechial rash
precautions: Medical emergency; meningococcal vaccine; prophylaxis for close contacts (Rifampicin)

symptoms: Muscle rigidity, trismus (lockjaw), spasms, autonomic dysfunction
precautions: Wound debridement; ICU care often needed; tetanus vaccine (TT/Tdap) prevention

symptoms: Paroxysmal cough with inspiratory 'whoop', post-tussive vomiting, cyanosis
precautions: Isolate patient; DTaP/Tdap vaccination; prophylaxis for household contacts

symptoms: Circular scaly itchy rash with clear center, hair loss (scalp)
precautions: Keep area dry; avoid sharing personal items; wash clothing/bedding

symptoms: White patches (oral), vaginal itching/discharge, skin rash in folds
precautions: Keep skin dry; manage blood sugar in diabetics; probiotics may help

symptoms: Headache, dizziness, blurred vision, nosebleeds (often asymptomatic)
precautions: Low-sodium diet (<2g/day), exercise, reduce alcohol, monitor BP daily

symptoms: Chest pain/tightness, exertional dyspnea, palpitations, fatigue
precautions: Smoking cessation, cardiac diet, regular exercise, stress management

symptoms: Dyspnea, orthopnea, bilateral leg edema, fatigue, reduced exercise tolerance
precautions: Fluid restriction (<1.5L/day), daily weight monitoring, low-sodium diet

symptoms: Palpitations, irregular pulse, dyspnea, dizziness, fatigue
precautions: Avoid caffeine/alcohol triggers; monitor INR if on Warfarin; fall precautions

symptoms: Unilateral leg swelling, redness, warmth, calf pain
precautions: Leg elevation, compression stockings, early ambulation, hydration

symptoms: Sudden dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, hemoptysis, tachycardia, hypoxia
precautions: Medical emergency; absolute rest initially; anticoagulation compliance critical

symptoms: Usually asymptomatic; may cause xanthelasma, corneal arcus
precautions: Low-fat diet, regular exercise, avoid trans fats, monitor LFTs

symptoms: Chest pressure/tightness on exertion, jaw/arm radiation, relieved by rest
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: Avoid triggers, carry GTN always, seek ER if pain >20 min (possible MI)

symptoms: Claudication, leg pain at rest, non-healing ulcers, cold extremities
precautions: Smoking cessation (critical), wound care, supervised walking program

symptoms: Post-MI: fatigue, reduced exercise capacity (acute: crushing chest pain)
precautions: Cardiac rehab, smoking cessation, dietary changes, psychological support

symptoms: Wheeze, breathlessness, chest tightness, nocturnal cough
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: Avoid triggers (dust, smoke, allergens); have rescue inhaler always; annual review

symptoms: Progressive dyspnea, chronic productive cough, wheeze, reduced exercise tolerance
precautions: Smoking cessation (most important); pulmonary rehab; annual influenza vaccine

symptoms: Sneezing, nasal congestion, watery rhinorrhea, itchy eyes/nose
precautions: Avoid allergens; saline nasal irrigation; allergy testing may guide treatment

symptoms: Facial pain/pressure, nasal congestion, purulent discharge, headache
precautions: Steam inhalation, saline rinses, avoid cold air; most cases viral – no antibiotics

symptoms: Productive cough, low-grade fever, wheeze, chest discomfort
medicine: Usually supportive: Paracetamol 500mg + Salbutamol inhaler (if wheeze)
precautions: Hydration, humidifier, honey-lemon; antibiotics only if bacterial superinfection

symptoms: Dyspnea on exertion, fatigue, syncope, right heart failure signs
precautions: Avoid high altitude, excessive exertion; O2 supplementation; specialist follow-up

symptoms: Chronic productive cough, recurrent lung infections, malabsorption, failure to thrive
precautions: Chest physiotherapy, high-calorie diet, enzyme supplements, annual reviews

symptoms: Progressive dyspnea, dry cough, bilateral crackles, finger clubbing
precautions: Pulmonary rehab, O2 therapy, avoid lung toxins; lung transplant evaluation

symptoms: Loud snoring, witnessed apneas, daytime somnolence, morning headache
medicine: CPAP therapy (primary) + Weight loss; Modafinil 200mg (if residual sleepiness)
precautions: Weight reduction, positional therapy, avoid alcohol/sedatives before bed

symptoms: Fever, pleuritic chest pain, dyspnea, pleural effusion on imaging
medicine: Isoniazid + Rifampicin + Pyrazinamide + Ethambutol (same as TB)
precautions: Same as pulmonary TB; therapeutic thoracocentesis if large effusion; pyridoxine supplement

symptoms: Polydipsia, polyuria, weight loss, fatigue, DKA in acute cases
precautions: Carbohydrate counting, glucose monitoring, sick-day rules, hypoglycemia management kit

symptoms: Often asymptomatic early; polyuria, fatigue, blurred vision, recurrent infections
precautions: Low glycemic index diet, regular exercise, weight loss, monitor HbA1c every 3 months

symptoms: Fatigue, weight gain, cold intolerance, constipation, bradycardia, dry skin
precautions: Take 30–60 min before breakfast; avoid calcium/iron supplements within 4 hours; monitor TSH

symptoms: Weight loss, palpitations, heat intolerance, tremor, exophthalmos (Graves')
precautions: Avoid iodine-rich foods; monitor thyroid function; consider radioiodine/surgery for relapse

symptoms: Moon face, central obesity, purple striae, hypertension, hyperglycemia
medicine: Ketoconazole 200mg (medical management) or surgical if adenoma
precautions: Investigate cause (exogenous vs endogenous); taper exogenous steroids gradually

symptoms: Fatigue, hyperpigmentation, hypotension, weight loss, salt craving
precautions: Carry steroid emergency card; sick-day rules (double dose); medical alert bracelet

symptoms: Irregular periods, hirsutism, acne, weight gain, infertility
precautions: Weight loss (5–10% improves symptoms), low GI diet, regular exercise

symptoms: Often asymptomatic; fragility fractures (wrist, hip, vertebra), back pain
precautions: Weight-bearing exercise, fall prevention, adequate protein intake, avoid smoking/alcohol

symptoms: Sudden severe joint pain (often big toe), redness, swelling, warmth
precautions: Low-purine diet (avoid red meat, alcohol, seafood), adequate hydration (>2L/day)

symptoms: BMI >30, dyspnea on exertion, sleep apnea, joint pain, psychosocial impact
precautions: Caloric deficit diet, regular exercise (150+ min/week), behavioral therapy, sleep hygiene

symptoms: Heartburn, regurgitation, belching, dysphagia, nocturnal cough, hoarseness
precautions: Avoid lying down after meals, elevate bed head, avoid trigger foods (coffee, spicy, fatty)

symptoms: Epigastric pain (hunger pain / night pain), bloating, nausea, hematemesis
precautions: Avoid NSAIDs, aspirin, alcohol, smoking; test and treat H. pylori

symptoms: Epigastric discomfort, nausea, bloating, loss of appetite
precautions: Avoid NSAIDs, alcohol, spicy food, coffee; small frequent meals

symptoms: Abdominal pain, bloating, alternating diarrhea/constipation, mucus in stool
precautions: Low-FODMAP diet, stress management, regular mealtimes, probiotic trial

symptoms: Abdominal cramping, chronic diarrhea (may be bloody), weight loss, perianal disease
precautions: No smoking (worsens Crohn's); regular colonoscopy; nutrition support if malnourished

symptoms: Bloody diarrhea, tenesmus, urgency, cramping, fatigue, anemia
precautions: Regular colonoscopy for cancer screening; stress management; avoid NSAIDs

symptoms: Infrequent stools (<3/week), straining, hard stools, incomplete evacuation
medicine: Lactulose 15ml OR Bisacodyl 5mg (short-term) OR Ispaghula husk
precautions: High-fiber diet, 2L water daily, regular exercise, respond to urge promptly

symptoms: Loose watery stools, abdominal cramps, nausea, risk of dehydration
frequency: PRN ORS / after each loose stool
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: AVOID Loperamide in bloody/febrile diarrhea; food hygiene; hand washing

symptoms: Rectal bleeding (bright red), perianal itching, prolapse, anal discomfort
precautions: High-fiber diet, adequate fluids, avoid straining, sitz baths, weight management

symptoms: Severe epigastric pain radiating to back, nausea, vomiting, elevated amylase/lipase
medicine: IV fluids + Tramadol 50–100mg (analgesia) + NPO initially then gradual feeding
precautions: Avoid alcohol absolutely; low-fat diet; treat gallstones; monitor blood glucose

symptoms: Seizures (tonic-clonic, absence, focal), post-ictal confusion, tongue biting
precautions: Never stop medication abruptly; avoid triggers (alcohol, sleep deprivation); driving restrictions

symptoms: Unilateral throbbing headache, nausea, photophobia, phonophobia, aura
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: Identify and avoid triggers (hormones, stress, foods); headache diary; dark quiet room during attack

symptoms: Resting tremor, rigidity, bradykinesia, postural instability, micrographia
precautions: Physiotherapy, occupational therapy, fall prevention, speech therapy; DBS if advanced

symptoms: Progressive memory loss, language problems, disorientation, behavioral changes
precautions: Cognitive stimulation, safe environment, carer support, advance care planning

symptoms: Optic neuritis, diplopia, weakness, sensory changes, ataxia, fatigue
medicine: Interferon beta-1a 30mcg IM (relapsing) OR Ocrelizumab 600mg IV (PPMS)
precautions: Physiotherapy, fatigue management, temperature awareness, psychological support

symptoms: Burning, shooting, electric shock-like pain; allodynia, hyperalgesia
precautions: Pain diary; TENS therapy; address underlying cause; sleep hygiene; psychological support

symptoms: Sudden, severe electric shock-like facial pain triggered by chewing/talking/touch
precautions: Avoid triggers (cold wind, chewing hard foods); surgical MVD if medication fails

symptoms: Irresistible urge to move legs, worse at rest/evening, creeping/crawling sensations
precautions: Iron supplementation if ferritin <75; avoid caffeine/alcohol; regular exercise; leg stretches

symptoms: Action tremor (hands), head nodding, voice tremor; worse with voluntary movement
precautions: Avoid caffeine; weighted utensils; avoid stress; alcohol transiently helps but avoid dependency

symptoms: Ptosis, diplopia, dysarthria, dysphagia, proximal limb weakness, fatigable muscle weakness
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon), 6:00 PM (Evening) & 10:00 PM (Night, before bed)
precautions: Avoid aminoglycosides, beta-blockers, fluoroquinolones (worsen MG); myasthenic crisis management

symptoms: Persistent low mood, anhedonia, fatigue, sleep disturbance, hopelessness, suicidal ideation
precautions: Psychotherapy (CBT), regular exercise, social support, safety plan if SI; adequate sleep

symptoms: Excessive worry, tension, restlessness, fatigue, sleep disturbance, irritability
precautions: CBT (gold standard), mindfulness, regular exercise, limit caffeine; relaxation techniques

symptoms: Mania: elevated mood, decreased need for sleep; Depression: low mood, hopelessness; psychosis
precautions: Monitor lithium levels; avoid alcohol; regular sleep schedule; early warning sign recognition

symptoms: Hallucinations, delusions, disorganized thinking, negative symptoms (alogia, avolition)
precautions: Medication adherence is critical; psychoeducation; community mental health support; occupational therapy

symptoms: Intrusive unwanted thoughts (obsessions) + repetitive behaviors (compulsions) to reduce anxiety
precautions: ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention) therapy is most effective; avoid OCD-accommodating behaviors

symptoms: Recurrent unexpected panic attacks: palpitations, sweating, trembling, derealization, fear of dying
precautions: CBT (panic-focused), breathing exercises, avoid caffeine; benzodiazepine dependence risk

symptoms: Flashbacks, nightmares, hypervigilance, avoidance, emotional numbing, sleep disturbance
precautions: Trauma-focused CBT, EMDR therapy; avoid substance use; safety and stability first

symptoms: Inattention, hyperactivity, impulsivity, poor organization, forgetfulness, low frustration tolerance
precautions: Behavioral therapy, structured routines, classroom/work accommodations; monitor growth in children

symptoms: Difficulty falling/staying asleep, early awakening, daytime fatigue, impaired functioning
medicine: Melatonin 2–5mg (short-term) OR Zolpidem 5mg (short-term only, max 2 weeks)
precautions: CBT-I (gold standard); sleep hygiene (no screens, regular schedule); avoid caffeine after 2pm

symptoms: Fear of social situations, blushing, trembling, sweating, avoidance, anticipatory anxiety
precautions: CBT with exposure therapy; graduated exposure hierarchy; social skills training

symptoms: Symmetric joint swelling/pain/morning stiffness (>1hr), fatigue, nodules
precautions: Monitor CBC/LFTs monthly; avoid live vaccines; folic acid reduces MTX side effects

symptoms: Butterfly rash, photosensitivity, arthralgia, renal disease, serositis, hair loss
precautions: Strict sun protection (SPF50+); monitor organ involvement; avoid estrogen OCP in active disease

symptoms: Well-demarcated silvery scaly plaques, scalp/elbow/knee involvement, nail pitting
precautions: Emollient use, avoid trauma (Koebner phenomenon), phototherapy, stress reduction

symptoms: Arthritis (DIP joints), dactylitis, enthesitis, nail changes, psoriatic skin lesions
precautions: Monitor joint damage with X-ray; physiotherapy; smoking cessation; weight management

symptoms: Inflammatory back pain (worse with rest, better with exercise), sacroiliac joint pain, stiffness
precautions: Daily spinal exercise/physiotherapy critical; sleep prone; posture awareness; avoid smoking

symptoms: Dry eyes, dry mouth, fatigue, parotid swelling, arthralgia
precautions: Regular dental care (caries risk); sip water frequently; avoid antihistamines/diuretics

symptoms: Fever, weight loss, organ-specific features (rash, hematuria, sinusitis, mononeuritis multiplex)
precautions: Monitor ANCA; renal function; infection screening before immunosuppression; BP control

symptoms: Proximal muscle weakness, heliotrope rash, Gottron's papules, dysphagia
precautions: Malignancy screening; physiotherapy; sun protection; monitor muscle enzymes (CK, LDH)

symptoms: Raynaud's, skin thickening, dysphagia, ILD, pulmonary hypertension, renal crisis
precautions: Hand warmers for Raynaud's; monitor BP (renal crisis); physiotherapy; esophageal dilation

symptoms: Bilateral shoulder/hip girdle pain and stiffness (>45 min morning stiffness), elderly patients
precautions: Calcium + Vitamin D supplementation; bone protection; monitor ESR/CRP; screen for GCA

symptoms: Comedones (blackheads/whiteheads), papules, pustules, nodules, scarring
medicine: Benzoyl peroxide 5% gel (mild) OR Adapalene 0.1% (retinoid) + Clindamycin 1% gel
precautions: Avoid picking/squeezing; gentle non-comedogenic skincare; sunscreen with topical retinoids

symptoms: Intensely itchy, dry, inflamed skin; eczematous patches; worse with triggers
medicine: Emollient (multiple daily) + Hydrocortisone 1% (flare) OR Tacrolimus 0.1% (sensitive areas)
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon) & 9:00 PM (Night) (primary medicine); see notes for others
precautions: Avoid triggers (soaps, dust mites, stress); wet wrap therapy; antihistamine for itch at night

symptoms: Often asymptomatic (open angle); acute angle closure: severe eye pain, headache, halos, nausea
medicine: Timolol 0.5% eye drops + Latanoprost 0.005% eye drops
precautions: Regular eye pressure monitoring; compliance critical (no symptoms = still taking drops); driving check

symptoms: Weak urinary stream, hesitancy, frequency, nocturia, incomplete bladder emptying
medicine: Tamsulosin 0.4mg (alpha-blocker) OR Finasteride 5mg (5-ARI for large gland)
precautions: Limit fluids before bedtime; avoid antihistamines/decongestants; bladder training exercises

symptoms: Urgency, urge urinary incontinence, frequency >8x/day, nocturia
precautions: Bladder training, pelvic floor exercises, fluid management (not restriction), reduce caffeine

symptoms: Inability to achieve/maintain erection sufficient for sexual intercourse
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: Treat cardiovascular risk factors; psychosexual therapy; avoid with nitrates (hypotension risk)

symptoms: Fatigue, pallor, exertional dyspnea, koilonychia (spoon nails), pica
precautions: Take on empty stomach or with Vitamin C for better absorption; avoid tea/coffee within 1hr; treat cause

symptoms: Bone pain, muscle weakness, fatigue, depression, recurrent infections
medicine: Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3) 1000–4000 IU/day OR weekly high-dose
precautions: Sun exposure 15–30 min daily; dietary sources (oily fish, eggs); adequate calcium co-supplementation

symptoms: Sneezing, runny/itchy nose, watery/red eyes, congestion, post-nasal drip (seasonal pattern)
medicine: Cetirizine 10mg OR Fexofenadine 120mg + Fluticasone nasal spray 50mcg
precautions: Monitor pollen count, keep windows closed during high pollen; shower after outdoor activities; sunglasses

symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, dizziness, pallor, cold sweats triggered by motion/travel
precautions: Sit in front seat/boat center; focus on horizon; avoid reading; fresh air; ginger (natural option)

symptoms: Vaso-occlusive pain crises, hemolytic anemia, dactylitis, acute chest syndrome, stroke risk, splenomegaly
precautions: Hydration (3L/day), avoid cold/hypoxia, annual transcranial Doppler, penicillin prophylaxis in children

symptoms: Severe anemia (major), pallor, splenomegaly, skeletal deformities, growth retardation, jaundice
medicine: Deferoxamine (iron chelation) 20–40mg/kg SC OR Deferasirox 20mg/kg oral + Folic acid
precautions: Regular blood transfusions (major); monitor ferritin/iron; genetic counseling for family

symptoms: Fatigue, pallor, petechiae, easy bruising, recurrent infections, bleeding gums
precautions: Avoid NSAIDs/aspirin; infection prevention; BMT if severe & young; growth factor support

symptoms: Prolonged bleeding after injury, spontaneous hemarthroses, deep muscle hematomas, intracranial bleed risk
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: AVOID aspirin/NSAIDs; wear medical alert bracelet; physiotherapy for joint disease; inhibitor screening

symptoms: Similar to Hemophilia A: prolonged bleeding, hemarthroses, muscle hematomas
medicine: Factor IX concentrate 25–50 IU/kg (on demand) OR Emicizumab SC (if inhibitors)
precautions: Same precautions as Hemophilia A; emicizumab is SC once weekly/fortnightly alternative

symptoms: Plethoric facies, pruritus after bathing, headache, dizziness, thrombosis risk, splenomegaly
precautions: Monitor CBC regularly; adequate hydration; avoid iron supplements; JAK inhibitor (Ruxolitinib) if refractory

symptoms: Headache, visual changes, tingling, thrombosis (arterial/venous), bleeding, splenomegaly
medicine: Hydroxyurea 500mg + Aspirin 75mg (low-risk may need only aspirin)
precautions: Monitor platelet count; cardiovascular risk factor control; avoid pregnancy on Hydroxyurea

symptoms: Petechiae, easy bruising, mucosal bleeding, menorrhagia; thrombocytopenia on CBC
precautions: Avoid NSAIDs; fall/trauma precautions; monitor platelet count; rituximab/splenectomy if chronic refractory

symptoms: Megaloblastic anemia, glossitis, subacute combined degeneration of cord, fatigue, paresthesia
medicine: Hydroxocobalamin 1mg IM (loading) then maintenance + Folic acid 5mg
frequency: Loading: alternate days x6 / Maintenance: every 3 months (IM)
precautions: Vegan diet supplementation; monitor neurological status; gastric cancer risk – endoscopy screening

symptoms: Jaundice, pallor, dark urine (hemoglobinuria), splenomegaly, fatigue, gallstones
precautions: Identify and treat underlying cause; avoid triggers (G6PD: oxidant drugs); Rituximab if refractory

symptoms: Fatigue, edema, hypertension, nausea, pallor, oliguria, pruritus in advanced stages
medicine: Ramipril 5–10mg + Furosemide 40mg + Sevelamer 800mg (phosphate binder) + Erythropoietin (if anemic)
precautions: Protein restriction (0.8g/kg/day), low potassium/phosphate diet, fluid restriction, BP <130/80, smoking cessation

symptoms: Oliguria/anuria, fluid retention, elevated creatinine/BUN, electrolyte disturbances, encephalopathy
medicine: IV Fluids (crystalloid) + Furosemide 40–80mg IV (if fluid overload) + treat underlying cause
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: Monitor urine output hourly; strict I/O chart; avoid nephrotoxins (NSAIDs, contrast, aminoglycosides)

symptoms: Severe proteinuria, pitting edema, hypoalbuminemia, hyperlipidemia, frothy urine
precautions: Low-salt low-fat diet; albumin IV if severe; anticoagulation if albumin <20g/L; infection vigilance

symptoms: Hematuria (cola-colored urine), hypertension, oliguria, proteinuria, edema
precautions: Identify cause (post-streptococcal, IgA, ANCA); renal biopsy may be needed; restrict salt/fluid

symptoms: Severe colicky flank/groin pain (loin to groin), hematuria, nausea, vomiting, dysuria
medicine: Diclofenac 75mg OR Ketorolac 30mg (analgesia) + Tamsulosin 0.4mg (alpha-blocker for passage)
precautions: High fluid intake (>2.5L/day); dietary oxalate restriction (calcium oxalate stones); citrate supplements

symptoms: Dysuria, frequency, urgency, suprapubic pain, cloudy/foul-smelling urine; no fever (uncomplicated)
precautions: Increase fluid intake; post-coital voiding; wipe front to back; avoid spermicides; cranberry as adjunct

symptoms: Fever, rigors, flank pain, costovertebral angle tenderness, dysuria, nausea/vomiting
medicine: Ciprofloxacin 500mg OR Co-amoxiclav 625mg (oral, outpatient) / Ceftriaxone 1g IV (inpatient)
precautions: High fluid intake; urine culture-guided therapy; imaging if no improvement in 48–72 hrs

symptoms: Chronic pelvic pain, urinary urgency/frequency, dyspareunia, no proven infection
precautions: Bladder training; avoid trigger foods (caffeine, acidic foods, alcohol); stress management; pelvic floor physio

symptoms: Urine leakage with coughing, sneezing, laughing, exercise; no urgency
medicine: Duloxetine 40mg (pharmacological) — first-line is pelvic floor exercises
precautions: Pelvic floor (Kegel) exercises (gold standard – 3 sets x 10 daily); weight loss; caffeine reduction; bladder diary

symptoms: Muscle weakness, palpitations, peaked T-waves on ECG, nausea; cardiac arrest if severe
medicine: Calcium gluconate 10% IV (cardiac protection) + Insulin + Dextrose (shift K⁺) + Sodium zirconium cyclosilicate 10g
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon) & 9:00 PM (Night) (primary medicine); see notes for others
precautions: STOP ACE inhibitors/ARBs/potassium-sparing diuretics; low-potassium diet; avoid NSAIDs; dialysis if severe

symptoms: Often asymptomatic; RUQ discomfort, fatigue, hepatomegaly; elevated ALT/AST
medicine: No specific drug approved — Vitamin E 800IU (non-diabetic NASH) + Lifestyle modification
precautions: Weight loss (>5–10%), exercise 150 min/week, avoid alcohol, control diabetes/lipids

symptoms: Fatigue, RUQ pain, hepatomegaly; elevated LFTs; can progress to cirrhosis
medicine: Vitamin E 800IU + Pioglitazone 30mg (if T2DM) + Semaglutide (off-label, emerging)
precautions: Same as fatty liver; biopsy-confirmed staging guides therapy; statin safe for lipids

symptoms: Jaundice, ascites, spider nevi, palmar erythema, caput medusae, hepatic encephalopathy, variceal bleeding
precautions: Absolute alcohol abstinence; low-sodium diet (<2g/day); monitor for HCC (US every 6 months); vaccination (Hep A/B)

symptoms: Fatigue, jaundice, arthralgia, elevated transaminases, elevated IgG, positive ANA/ASMA
precautions: Monitor LFTs and CBC; avoid alcohol; screen for osteoporosis (long-term steroid); check TPMT before Azathioprine

symptoms: Pruritus, fatigue, jaundice (late), xanthelasma, osteoporosis; positive AMA
medicine: Ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA) 13–15mg/kg/day + Obeticholic acid 5mg (if inadequate UDCA response)
precautions: Fat-soluble vitamin supplementation (A,D,E,K); cholestyramine for pruritus; bone density monitoring

symptoms: Fatigue, pruritus, jaundice, RUQ pain, recurrent cholangitis, associated IBD
medicine: No proven disease-modifying drug; UDCA 15–20mg/kg (controversial) + Ciprofloxacin (cholangitis episodes)
precautions: Annual colonoscopy (IBD/CRC risk); MRCP surveillance; liver transplant evaluation; cholangiocarcinoma screening

symptoms: Often asymptomatic; biliary colic (RUQ pain after fatty meals), nausea, vomiting
medicine: Ursodeoxycholic acid 8–10mg/kg (for small cholesterol stones – dissolution) + Diclofenac 75mg (analgesia for colic)
precautions: Low-fat diet; surgical cholecystectomy preferred for symptomatic stones; avoid rapid weight loss

symptoms: Severe RUQ pain, fever, positive Murphy's sign, nausea, jaundice if obstructed
precautions: NPO initially; IV fluids; cholecystectomy (definitive); avoid fatty foods; analgesia with diclofenac

symptoms: Confusion, asterixis (flapping tremor), altered consciousness, fetor hepaticus, hyperammonemia
precautions: Identify precipitants (infection, GI bleed, constipation, diuretics); protein-adequate diet; monitor ammonia

symptoms: Liver disease (hepatitis/cirrhosis), Kayser-Fleischer rings, neuropsychiatric symptoms, hemolytic anemia
medicine: D-Penicillamine 250–500mg (initial chelation) OR Trientine 300–400mg (if intolerant) + Zinc acetate 50mg (maintenance)
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon), 6:00 PM (Evening) & 10:00 PM (Night, before bed)
precautions: Low copper diet (avoid shellfish, liver, chocolate, nuts); monitor 24hr urine copper; eye exams; pyridoxine with D-Penicillamine

symptoms: Blurred/cloudy vision, glare/halos around lights, faded colors, frequent prescription changes
medicine: Artificial tears (lubricant drops) — no medication reverses cataracts; surgical extraction is definitive
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon), 6:00 PM (Evening) & 10:00 PM (Night, before bed)
precautions: Strong reading glasses, anti-glare lenses, adequate lighting; avoid driving at night; plan surgical referral

symptoms: Red eye, discharge (watery-viral / purulent-bacterial), itching, foreign body sensation, crusting
medicine: Chloramphenicol 0.5% drops (bacterial) OR Olopatadine 0.1% (allergic) / Viral: supportive only
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon), 6:00 PM (Evening) & 10:00 PM (Night, before bed)
precautions: Hand hygiene (highly contagious); avoid contact lenses; do not share towels/pillowcases

symptoms: Gritty/burning sensation, intermittent blurry vision, light sensitivity, excessive tearing (reflex)
medicine: Carboxymethylcellulose 0.5% drops (artificial tears) + Cyclosporine 0.05% drops (Restasis, chronic)
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon), 6:00 PM (Evening) & 10:00 PM (Night, before bed)
precautions: Screen breaks (20-20-20 rule), humidifier, omega-3 supplements, avoid smoky environments

symptoms: Red painful eye, photophobia, blurred vision, floaters; ciliary flush; hypopyon in severe cases
medicine: Prednisolone acetate 1% eye drops + Cyclopentolate 1% (mydriatic to prevent synechiae)
time: Every hour as directed (acute phase) — then taper
precautions: Urgent ophthalmic referral; systemic workup for underlying cause (HLA-B27, TB, sarcoid, syphilis)

symptoms: Severe eye pain, photophobia, lacrimation, reduced vision, corneal opacity/infiltrate
medicine: Ofloxacin 0.3% drops (bacterial) OR Natamycin 5% (fungal) OR Acyclovir eye ointment (HSV)
time: Every hour as directed (acute phase) — then taper
precautions: Ophthalmologic emergency; stop contact lens use; avoid steroid drops until cause confirmed

symptoms: Lid margin redness/crusting, gritty eyes, morning lid sticking, recurrent styes, loss of eyelashes
medicine: Lid hygiene (warm compress + lid scrubs) + Chloramphenicol ointment 1% (if bacterial) + Artificial tears
precautions: Warm compresses 5–10 min daily followed by lid massage and lid scrubs; omega-3 supplements help

symptoms: Blurred vision, floaters, dark spots, visual field loss (late); often asymptomatic early
medicine: Ranibizumab/Bevacizumab intravitreal injections (DME/PDR) + strict glycemic control
precautions: Strict HbA1c <7%, BP <130/80; annual dilated fundoscopy; laser photocoagulation; stop smoking

symptoms: Central vision loss, metamorphopsia (distorted vision), scotoma, difficulty reading
medicine: Ranibizumab 0.5mg OR Aflibercept 2mg intravitreal injections (wet AMD); Supplements: AREDS2 (dry AMD)
precautions: Amsler grid self-monitoring; smoking cessation (major risk factor); sunglasses with UV protection; low vision aids

symptoms: Elevated IOP (>21 mmHg) without optic nerve damage or visual field loss; often asymptomatic
precautions: Regular IOP monitoring (every 6 months); visual field testing annually; compliance is critical

symptoms: Sudden painless visual loss/blurring (central) or sector visual field loss (branch); flame hemorrhages on fundus
medicine: Ranibizumab/Bevacizumab intravitreal injections (macular edema) + treat systemic risk factors
precautions: Control BP, diabetes, hyperlipidemia; aspirin 75mg; screen for thrombophilia; no proven acute treatment

symptoms: Ear pain, fever, hearing loss, irritability (children), otorrhea if perforated
medicine: Amoxicillin 500mg (if bacterial) / Watchful waiting first 48–72hrs in mild cases + Paracetamol
precautions: Analgesia first; avoid water entry; decongestants not recommended in children; referral if recurrent (>3/6 months)

symptoms: Severe ear pain (worse on pinna traction), itching, discharge, conductive hearing loss
medicine: Acetic acid 2% spray OR Sofradex drops (dexamethasone + framycetin + gramicidin)
precautions: Keep ear dry (cotton wool with Vaseline when showering); avoid cotton buds; ear wick if canal swollen

symptoms: Episodic vertigo (minutes to hours), unilateral sensorineural hearing loss, tinnitus, aural fullness
precautions: Low-salt diet (<1.5g/day); avoid caffeine/alcohol; vestibular rehab; fall safety during attacks

symptoms: Persistent ringing/buzzing/hissing in ears; may cause sleep disturbance, anxiety, concentration issues
medicine: No curative drug; Betahistine 8–16mg (trial) + Melatonin 2mg (sleep) + Cognitive support
precautions: Sound therapy (white noise); TRT (Tinnitus Retraining Therapy); hearing aids if hearing loss; avoid loud noise

symptoms: Hoarseness, aphonia, dry cough, sore/scratchy throat, odynophagia; low-grade fever (infective)
medicine: Supportive: Paracetamol 500mg + steam inhalation; Omeprazole if LPR related
precautions: Voice rest (do not whisper); stay hydrated; avoid irritants (smoking, alcohol); humidified air; ENT if >3 weeks

symptoms: Sore throat, dysphagia, swollen tonsils with exudate, fever, cervical lymphadenopathy
medicine: Amoxicillin 500mg (bacterial/Group A Strep) OR Clarithromycin (if penicillin allergic) + Paracetamol
precautions: AVOID Ampicillin/Amoxicillin if mononucleosis suspected (causes rash); tonsillectomy if recurrent (≥7/yr)

symptoms: Sore throat, odynophagia, fever, pharyngeal erythema; mostly viral
medicine: Paracetamol 500mg + Benzydamine spray (topical analgesic) / Amoxicillin if Centor score ≥3
precautions: Gargle warm salt water; adequate hydration; throat swab to guide antibiotic use (avoid overuse)

symptoms: Bilateral nasal obstruction, anosmia, rhinorrhea, post-nasal drip; associated with asthma/aspirin sensitivity
medicine: Mometasone furoate nasal spray 200mcg/day + Dupilumab 300mg SC (if biologic eligible)
precautions: Avoid aspirin/NSAIDs (if NSAID-exacerbated respiratory disease); saline irrigation; surgical polypectomy if unresponsive

symptoms: Medical emergency: rapid onset high fever, stridor, drooling, dysphagia, 'tripod' posture, muffled voice
precautions: DO NOT examine throat without airway support; secure airway first; Hib vaccine prevention

symptoms: Sudden severe vertigo, nausea/vomiting, gait instability; no hearing loss; follows viral URTI
medicine: Prochlorperazine 5mg (acute vestibular sedative) + Prednisolone 50mg (early, if within 72hrs)
precautions: Vestibular rehabilitation exercises (Cawthorne-Cooksey); early mobilization; fall precautions; avoid driving during acute phase

symptoms: Facial flushing, persistent erythema, papulopustules, telangiectasia, rhinophyma (late); triggered by sun/heat/alcohol
medicine: Metronidazole 0.75% gel + Azelaic acid 15% gel (topical) / Doxycycline 40mg (papulopustular)
precautions: Sun protection (SPF50+ daily); avoid triggers (heat, alcohol, spicy food); gentle skincare; laser for erythema/telangiectasia

symptoms: Greasy yellowish scales on scalp/face/nasolabial folds, itching, erythema, dandruff
time: 8:00 AM (Morning) & 9:00 PM (Night) (primary medicine); see notes for others
precautions: Maintenance use 1–2x/month to prevent recurrence; avoid harsh soaps; stress management

symptoms: Depigmented white patches (hands, face, genitalia, bony prominences); cosmetically distressing; no systemic symptoms
medicine: Tacrolimus 0.1% ointment (face/folds) OR Clobetasol 0.05% cream (body) + Ruxolitinib 1.5% cream (newer topical JAK-i)
precautions: Strict sun protection of depigmented areas (burn easily); cosmetic camouflage; NB-UVB phototherapy; psychological support

symptoms: Recurrent painful nodules/abscesses in axillae/groin/inframammary areas, sinus tracts, scarring
medicine: Clindamycin 1% lotion (topical, mild) + Doxycycline 100mg (oral, moderate) / Adalimumab 40mg (severe, biologic)
precautions: Weight loss; stop smoking (significant trigger); warm compresses; antiseptic washes; surgical drainage if abscess

symptoms: Honey-colored crusted lesions (non-bullous) or fluid-filled bullae (bullous) around face/extremities
precautions: Keep lesions covered; good hand hygiene; avoid school/work until crusts cleared; clean shared items

symptoms: Unilateral spreading erythema, warmth, swelling, tenderness, fever; usually lower limbs
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon), 6:00 PM (Evening) & 10:00 PM (Night, before bed)
precautions: Mark erythema border to monitor spread; elevate limb; moisturize to prevent fissures; treat tinea pedis as portal

symptoms: Intense nocturnal pruritus, burrows (finger webs/wrists/genitalia), papular rash, secondary infection
medicine: Permethrin 5% cream (from neck down) + Oral Ivermectin 200mcg/kg (if extensive/crusted)
frequency: Single application (repeat at 7 days) / Single dose (repeat at 14 days)
precautions: Treat all household contacts simultaneously; wash all clothing/bedding at 60°C; vacuum furniture

symptoms: Transient itchy wheals (hives), angioedema, dermographism; acute <6 weeks / chronic >6 weeks
medicine: Cetirizine 10mg OR Fexofenadine 180mg (non-sedating first-line) / Omalizumab 300mg (chronic refractory)
precautions: Identify and avoid triggers; adrenaline auto-injector if risk of anaphylaxis; avoid aspirin/NSAIDs/ACE-inhibitors

symptoms: Erythema, vesicles, weeping, pruritus at site of contact (allergic) or irritant exposure; clear borders
medicine: Hydrocortisone 1% cream (mild) OR Betamethasone valerate 0.1% (moderate-severe) + Emollient
precautions: Identify and avoid causative allergen/irritant; patch testing; protective gloves; barrier cream

symptoms: Well-circumscribed patches of non-scarring hair loss; exclamation mark hairs at patch margins; nail pitting
medicine: Intralesional Triamcinolone 10mg/mL (patches) OR Topical Minoxidil 5% + Baricitinib 4mg (severe)
precautions: Psychological support; wigs/hairpieces; avoid mechanical trauma; spontaneous remission possible in 50%

symptoms: Severe dysmenorrhea, dyspareunia, chronic pelvic pain, dyschezia, infertility, menorrhagia
medicine: Combined OCP (continuous) OR Norethindrone acetate 5mg / Dienogest 2mg + NSAID (pain)
precautions: Laparoscopic diagnosis/treatment; pain diary; physiotherapy; surgical excision for severe disease/infertility

symptoms: Menorrhagia, pelvic pressure, urinary frequency/urgency, dysmenorrhea, subfertility
medicine: Tranexamic acid 1g (menorrhagia) + Ulipristal acetate (Esmya) 5mg OR GnRH agonist (Leuprorelin) pre-surgery
precautions: Iron supplementation for anemia; NSAIDs for pain; myomectomy/hysterectomy if severe; uterine artery embolization

symptoms: Lower abdominal pain, fever, cervical motion tenderness, mucopurulent discharge, dyspareunia
time: 8:00 AM (Morning) & 9:00 PM (Night) (primary medicine); see notes for others
precautions: Treat partner; avoid sex until course completed; IUCD removal if not settling; check for chlamydia/gonorrhea

symptoms: Heavy menstrual bleeding (>80ml/cycle), prolonged periods >7 days, flooding, passage of clots
medicine: Tranexamic acid 1g + Mefenamic acid 500mg (during menses) / Levonorgestrel-IUS (Mirena) long-term
precautions: Iron supplementation; treat underlying cause (fibroids, PCOS, coagulation disorders); endometrial biopsy if >40yrs

symptoms: Severe mood swings, irritability, depression, anxiety, bloating, breast tenderness, fatigue premenstrually
medicine: Sertraline 50mg (continuous or luteal phase only) OR Combined OCP (Drospirenone-containing)
precautions: Symptom diary (2+ cycles to confirm); regular exercise; calcium 1200mg/day supplements; CBT

symptoms: Vaginal discharge (colour/odour varies by cause), itching, burning, odour
medicine: Metronidazole 400mg (BV) OR Clotrimazole pessary (Candida) OR Doxycycline (atrophic: Estradiol cream)
precautions: Microscopy/pH testing to confirm cause; avoid douching; cotton underwear; treat partner for Trichomonas

symptoms: Thick white cottage-cheese discharge, intense vulval itching, burning, dyspareunia, erythema
precautions: Avoid tight synthetic clothing; control blood sugar (diabetics); probiotics; recurrent (>4/yr) needs prophylactic fluconazole

symptoms: Hot flushes, night sweats, vaginal dryness, mood changes, sleep disturbance, urinary symptoms
medicine: HRT: Estradiol patch 50mcg + Norethisterone 1mg (if uterus intact) / Vagifem vaginal tablets
frequency: Twice weekly patch + 1x/day oral / Vagifem 1x/day initially
precautions: Annual breast exam/mammogram; bone density monitoring; SSRIs (Venlafaxine) if HRT contraindicated; lifestyle measures

symptoms: Crampy lower abdominal pain at start of menstruation, radiation to back/thighs, nausea, diarrhea
medicine: Ibuprofen 400mg OR Mefenamic acid 500mg (start 1–2 days before period)
precautions: Heat application (hot water bottle); regular exercise; exclude secondary causes (endometriosis, fibroids); OCP effective for primary dysmenorrhea

symptoms: Inability to conceive after 12 months unprotected intercourse; PCOS, ovulatory dysfunction, hypothyroidism causes
medicine: Clomifene citrate 50–100mg (ovulation induction – PCOS/anovulation) OR Letrozole 2.5–5mg
duration_days: 5 days per cycle, up to 6 cycles
precautions: Optimize BMI; treat thyroid/hormonal disorders; folate 400mcg/day; lifestyle modification; referral for IUI/IVF

symptoms: Fever, oral ulcers, vesicular rash on hands/feet/buttocks; mainly in children <5 years; Coxsackievirus
medicine: Supportive: Paracetamol/Ibuprofen (fever/pain) + Oral gel (e.g., Lidocaine gel) for oral ulcers
precautions: Isolate from school/daycare; hand hygiene; cool fluids/ice lollies; monitor hydration; no specific antiviral

symptoms: Barking ('seal-like') cough, inspiratory stridor, hoarseness, low-grade fever; worse at night; under 5 years
medicine: Dexamethasone 0.15–0.6mg/kg oral (single dose) + Nebulized Budesonide (moderate-severe) / Nebulized Adrenaline (severe)
precautions: Cool mist/night air; calm the child (agitation worsens stridor); emergency if severe respiratory distress

symptoms: Runny nose, cough, wheeze, tachypnea, feeding difficulty, hypoxia; severe in infants <6 months
medicine: Supportive: Nasal saline drops + Suction + Paracetamol + Oxygen if SpO2 <92%
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: Palivizumab prophylaxis for high-risk infants; hand hygiene; breastfeeding protective; no bronchodilators in infants

symptoms: Sandpaper rash (trunk first), strawberry tongue, pharyngitis, fever, circumoral pallor, Pastia's lines
precautions: Notifiable disease; exclude from school for 24 hrs after starting antibiotics; treat to prevent rheumatic fever/glomerulonephritis

symptoms: Perianal/nocturnal itching, disturbed sleep, visible worms around anus at night; irritability
medicine: Mebendazole 100mg (>2yrs) OR Albendazole 400mg (single dose; repeat at 2 weeks)
precautions: Treat ALL household members simultaneously; wash bedding/underwear daily; cut fingernails short; hand hygiene

symptoms: Fever >5 days + 4 of: bilateral conjunctivitis, rash, oral changes (strawberry tongue), cervical lymphadenopathy, hand/foot changes
medicine: IVIG 2g/kg IV (single infusion) + Aspirin 30–50mg/kg/day (high dose, then reduce)
time: 8:00 AM (Morning), 1:00 PM (Afternoon), 6:00 PM (Evening) & 10:00 PM (Night, before bed) (primary medicine); see notes for others
precautions: Echocardiogram to assess coronary aneurysms; monitor ESR/CRP; long-term cardiology follow-up if coronary involvement

symptoms: Joint swelling/pain/morning stiffness (>6 weeks in children <16yrs); fever, rash (systemic JIA); uveitis risk
precautions: Annual slit-lamp exam (uveitis screening); physiotherapy; occupational therapy; school accommodations; growth monitoring

symptoms: Brief (<15 min) tonic-clonic seizure with fever in child 6 months–5 years; no CNS infection
medicine: Diazepam rectal 5–10mg (if seizure >5 min) / Midazolam buccal 0.3mg/kg + Paracetamol (fever management)
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: Lay child on side (recovery position); do NOT restrain; fever management; LP to exclude meningitis if <18 months; parental education

symptoms: Infrequent hard stools (<3/week), straining, painful defecation, abdominal pain, soiling (overflow incontinence)
precautions: High-fiber diet, adequate fluids (for age), regular toilet routine after meals, positive reinforcement; exclude Hirschsprung's

symptoms: Wheeze, cough (worse at night/exercise), breathlessness, chest tightness; episodic; triggered by URTI/allergens
medicine: Salbutamol 100mcg inhaler (2–4 puffs via spacer, PRN) + Beclomethasone 50–100mcg (preventer)
time: As needed (PRN) — typically Morning or Night when symptoms occur
precautions: Use spacer always; written asthma action plan; trigger avoidance; annual review; influenza vaccine

symptoms: Painless breast lump, skin dimpling/peau d'orange, nipple discharge/inversion, axillary lymphadenopathy
medicine: Tamoxifen 20mg (premenopausal, hormone receptor +ve) OR Letrozole 2.5mg (postmenopausal) + Chemotherapy (as per protocol)
precautions: Breast self-examination; annual mammography; genetic testing (BRCA); bone density monitoring on AI; adherence critical

symptoms: Persistent cough, hemoptysis, dyspnea, weight loss, hoarseness, chest pain, Horner's syndrome
medicine: Erlotinib/Osimertinib (EGFR+) OR Pembrolizumab (high PD-L1) / Carboplatin + Paclitaxel (chemo)
precautions: Smoking cessation; palliative care integration; nutritional support; CT surveillance; molecular testing before treatment

symptoms: Change in bowel habit, rectal bleeding, abdominal pain, weight loss, iron-deficiency anemia, tenesmus
medicine: Capecitabine 1250mg/m² (adjuvant oral) OR FOLFOX/FOLFIRI (IV regimen) + Bevacizumab (metastatic)
precautions: Colonoscopy surveillance post-resection; high-fiber diet; CEA monitoring; genetic testing (Lynch syndrome)

symptoms: Often asymptomatic (early); urinary symptoms, bone pain (metastatic), elevated PSA
medicine: Enzalutamide 160mg OR Abiraterone 1000mg + Prednisolone 5mg (CRPC) / Leuprorelin 3-monthly SC (ADT)
precautions: Bone protection (Zoledronic acid, Denosumab); monitor PSA; bone density; cardiovascular risk on ADT

symptoms: Fatigue, pallor, recurrent infections, bruising/bleeding, lymphadenopathy, bone pain, splenomegaly
medicine: Imatinib 400mg (CML) / ATRA + Arsenic trioxide (APL) / R-CHOP (B-cell) / Daunorubicin + Cytarabine (AML induction)
precautions: Infection prevention (neutropenic precautions); mouth care; blood product support; fertility preservation before chemo

symptoms: Painless lymphadenopathy, B symptoms (fever, night sweats, weight loss), fatigue, pruritus
precautions: Infection prevention; G-CSF support; cardiac monitoring (anthracyclines); surveillance PET-CT; fertility preservation

symptoms: Bone pain (CRAB: hyperCalcemia, Renal failure, Anemia, Bone lesions), fatigue, recurrent infections
time: 8:00 AM (Morning) (Daily dose); 9:00 PM (Night) (Weekly doses on cycle days)
precautions: Bone protection (Zoledronic acid); VTE prophylaxis on Lenalidomide; infection prophylaxis; hydration for renal protection

symptoms: Abdominal bloating/distension, pelvic pain, early satiety, urinary frequency, weight changes, elevated CA-125
medicine: Carboplatin AUC5 + Paclitaxel 175mg/m² IV + Bevacizumab (advanced) / Olaparib (BRCA mutated maintenance)
precautions: CA-125 and CT monitoring; genetic counseling (BRCA); secondary surgical cytoreduction; nutritional support

symptoms: Neck lump, dysphagia, hoarseness, cervical lymphadenopathy; most are well-differentiated papillary
medicine: Levothyroxine (TSH suppression: 0.1–0.5 mU/L) post-thyroidectomy / Radioactive iodine (I-131) ablation
precautions: Post-RAI diet (low iodine before); annual thyroglobulin and neck ultrasound; Cabozantinib/Sorafenib for advanced

symptoms: Changing mole (ABCDE: Asymmetry, Border irregularity, Color variation, Diameter >6mm, Evolution), bleeding, pruritus
medicine: Pembrolizumab 200mg IV (adjuvant/advanced) OR BRAF+: Dabrafenib + Trametinib (targeted therapy)
precautions: Sun protection (SPF50+ always); annual full-body skin exam; dermatoscopy surveillance; sentinel lymph node biopsy

symptoms: Tall stature, arachnodactyly, lens dislocation, aortic root dilation/dissection risk, pectus excavatum, joint hypermobility
precautions: Annual echo (aortic root monitoring); avoid contact sports/strenuous exercise; ophthalmology follow-up; genetic counseling

symptoms: Chorea, cognitive decline, psychiatric symptoms (depression, irritability), dysphagia, weight loss; autosomal dominant
precautions: No disease-modifying treatment yet; multidisciplinary team care; genetic counseling for family; speech therapy; physiotherapy; advance care planning

symptoms: Progressive limb weakness, fasciculations, dysarthria, dysphagia, respiratory failure; UMN+LMN signs
medicine: Riluzole 50mg + Edaravone 60mg IV (infusion cycles) + symptomatic (Baclofen for spasticity)
precautions: Multidisciplinary team; PEG (feeding) when dysphagia severe; NIV for respiratory support; advance directives early; palliative care

symptoms: Acroparesthesia (burning pain in hands/feet), angiokeratomas, hypohidrosis, corneal verticillata, renal/cardiac involvement
medicine: Agalsidase beta (Fabrazyme) 1mg/kg IV infusion OR Migalastat 123mg (if amenable mutation)
precautions: Analgesics (carbamazepine) for pain crises; renal monitoring; cardiology; avoid triggers (heat, exercise); genetic counseling

symptoms: Splenomegaly, hepatomegaly, bone pain/crises, anemia, thrombocytopenia; Ashkenazi Jewish association
medicine: Imiglucerase (Cerezyme) IV infusion 60U/kg OR Eliglustat 84mg oral (substrate reduction therapy)
precautions: Monitor CBC, ferritin, chitotriosidase; orthopedic management (bone crises); splenectomy rarely needed now; genetic counseling

symptoms: Bone pain, bone deformity (bowed tibia, enlarged skull), warmth over bone, elevated ALP; may be asymptomatic
medicine: Zoledronic acid 5mg IV (single infusion – highly effective) OR Risedronate 30mg oral (alternative)
time: 8:00 AM (Morning) (primary medicine); see notes for others
precautions: Calcium 1000mg + Vitamin D 800IU supplementation; hearing assessment (skull involvement); monitor ALP to assess response

symptoms: Dyspnea, cough, bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy, erythema nodosum, uveitis, hypercalcemia, fatigue, lupus pernio
precautions: Many cases remit spontaneously; avoid calcium/vitamin D supplementation (hypercalcemia risk); organ-specific monitoring (lungs, eyes, heart)

symptoms: Recurrent oral ulcers (pathognomonic), genital ulcers, uveitis, skin lesions (pseudofolliculitis), arthritis, CNS involvement
precautions: Topical steroids for oral/genital ulcers; ophthalmology for uveitis; anticoagulation for vascular disease; avoid pathergy triggers

symptoms: Profound fatigue >6 months not relieved by rest, post-exertional malaise, cognitive impairment ('brain fog'), sleep disturbance, orthostatic intolerance
medicine: No curative drug; Low-dose Amitriptyline 10–25mg (sleep/pain) + Melatonin 2mg + Fludrocortisone 0.1mg (if POTS component)
precautions: Pacing (energy management – most important); avoid boom-bust cycle; graded activity (not aggressive GET); cognitive support; MDT approach; psychological support

symptoms: Widespread musculoskeletal pain, allodynia, fatigue, non-restorative sleep, cognitive difficulties, IBS, headache
precautions: Aerobic exercise (most evidence-based intervention); CBT; sleep hygiene; patient education; avoid opioids; multidisciplinary pain management
